author: @JK-zl7vv
text: Had you thought of using your cement flywheel, with your ball point pen low friction bearings,  also any type of a metal for your coil holder such as a bike rim, (steel or aluminium), will cause Eddie currents, so you may just want to use a plastic ring instead, to hold your windings.  I also saw a video where current was placed on the outer race of a bearing, a steel shaft was put through the center of the bearing to another bearing, and the output of the current was pick back up off of the 2nd bearing outer case, so with the current traveling from one bearing, through the shaft and out of the 2nd bearing, it caused the shaft to spin very fast, no magnets, no stator, no winding  or brushes, just a shaft and 2 bearings, so I wonder, if you run the current being created by your generator, through its bearings, if it would improve its efficiencies by a large margin.? 🤠👍

author: @ryanlebeck259
text:
You could generate a reasonable amount from ceramic kitchen magnets. If you split the flywheel into eight quadrants and did north south alternating by quadrant you could get strong alternating current. By bisecting the flywheel and having north on one side and south on the other you could effectively build a Hertz generator and have it be sine wave specific at a certain rpm.

Well done mate!

author: @Gwydion67
text:
Ok, used some magnifier to get the readings myself, then did the math, and voilà, came out with ~32¼mW frlm those 2 magnets configuration. Multiplied with 18 (=36 magnets, fully equipped) it should give a total of ~581mW (0.58W or 0.00058kW). Would be interesting to know how long the flywheel spins, or how much energy in terms of (m)Wh will be produced.
That was really fun, a nice demonstration of the principle. ⚙⚡👍
And up we go, right to the next episode...👋🏃‍♂️

author: @smokyatgroups
text: Hi Robert, maybe think of flipping the magnets by 90 degrees, (though it's not easy) so their NS / SN field comes out parallel with the flywheel's axle.  Then place a pickup coil above and below each magnet, if each coil pair is wired correctly, it will cancel out the Lenz effect deceleration force. Because the back EMF from load current is equal but opposite on each side of the same magnet.

author: @hanslepoeter5167

Starting with the little toy car with friction motor, flywheels in cars to store breaking energy, flywheels in all kind of electrical devices to keep power generation or usage stable. Flywheels have good applications in current modern technology.

author: @shedactivist
text: If the voltage is generated from the changing magnetic flux then putting the two magnets opposite on the wheel means that what you got was the absolute minimum. Two magnets together with a balance opposite may yield better results. That is one experiment I would do before adding more magnets.

author: @MiscDotGeek

author: @ArcanusLibero
text: Brilliant. Just Brilliant...I was reading up on industrial flywheel offerings and a great many of them can get four hours of spin once they reach optimum speed. Imagine the possibilities for an off grid system or backup system if you have battery storage or even without. If one could achieve a steady flow of power for 15-20 minutes with a minimum of physical input. Wow, what an accomplishment that would be... I am optimistic that on an individual level one should be able to achieve a percentage of the performance that highly developed flywheel manufacturers accomplish.

text:
I have been kicking something similar around in my head. I like the flywheel concept. 

I was thinking a row of the “flywheels” connected to what you have there. Say 3 or 5. Then a seated stationary bike hooked to your central sprocket with a gear shift derailer. Be amble to start it in low gear and work up as you get them all spinning. Make a great supplement to a wind, hydro, and solar setup. 

Keep up the great work.

author: @markg1051
text:
You need to connect an oscilloscope to that thing to get an idea of what the peak volts and amps you are getting as your voltmeter is most likely giving you avarage readings. This will give you a better idea of what you will be getting when you decorate the outer circumference with more magnets. If you do get a scope, for current measurement load the output with something like 0.1 ohm resistor and measure the peak voltage across it then the peak current will simply be peak volts divided by 0.1 ohms.
Love the "evil scientist" laugh.😁😎

author: @lanewooten3291
text: What a great idea! In the late '80s or early '90s, there was an article I read(I think it was in discover magazine) named "reinventing the wheel". The scientist or engineer fellow had developed a flywheel surrounded by magnets in a vacuum. In the article, he claimed that he could get the flywheel spinning up to give a car a 300-mile range per day. At night, at night wor whenever you could recharge the spin back up for the next day. Looks like great minds are thinking alike.
